title: Canonical benchmark gate repair and paired attribution
summary: Restore the canonical fixed-baseline full benchmark gate on clean main, pair exact-main and WM-0177 checkpoint evidence, and identify any exact sim/product repair scope without waiving or weakening performance requirements.
acceptance: The benchmark workload, fixtures, invariant payloads, packages/benchmarks/baseline.json medians, 10 percent warning threshold, 20 percent failure threshold, default 9 samples and 2 warmups remain immutable; no suite may be skipped, softened, retried-until-pass or reported through a waiver

Record raw per-sample evidence for clean main at 4175afd and WM-0177 checkpoint 054d501, including exact commit, Node and pnpm versions, Windows/architecture, CPU model/count, host identity or stable anonymized host key, sample/warmup counts, command, exit code and artifact hash

Run every comparison in an isolated Node process and alternate clean-main versus WM-0177 checkpoint runs for each filtered failing suite and for the full suite. Use the single existing worktree with serial clean checkouts or a reviewed equivalent; do not create a linked worktree and do not compare dirty trees

Reproduce and resolve the reviewed attribution: entity-store is a pre-existing clean-main gate failure; M3 ordinary-life, M5 alpha-content and spatial-index are WM-0177 task-associated regressions; M4 and map-dirty are mixed clean-main failures with additional task-branch worsening. Owner-local work-offers, pathing and reservations evidence may support attribution but cannot replace the aggregate gate

Repair only explicitly owned benchmark harness or benchmark-environment surfaces. The repair must preserve benchmark meaning and measured workload while making the clean-main lineage pass the exact corepack pnpm bench command against the unchanged canonical baseline

The final clean-main-lineage corepack pnpm bench run exits zero with every invariant matching and no comparison above the unchanged 20 percent failure threshold; warnings above 10 percent are documented with raw evidence and do not conceal a failure

If diagnosis requires any packages/sim-core, product, scenario-authority or other non-harness file, stop and taskctl block with exact files, call paths, suite evidence and reason. Systems architecture may create WM-0179 only from that structured blocker; WM-0179 does not exist and is not pre-approved by this packet

Historical benchmark artifacts, including coordination/artifacts/WM-0083, remain byte-identical to HEAD after each comparison. New raw and comparison evidence is written only under coordination/artifacts/WM-0178 and is covered by hashes

WM-0177 and WM-0170 remain blocked throughout WM-0178. WM-0177 may resume only after WM-0178 is independently verified, integrated and done and the repaired main full benchmark gate passes without a waiver
requiredChecks: corepack pnpm exec vitest run packages/benchmarks/src/baseline.test.ts packages/benchmarks/src/map-dirty-benchmark.test.ts

history.details: Approved a mandatory qa-performance predecessor for fixed-baseline canonical gate repair and isolated paired attribution. Workload, baseline, warning/failure thresholds and full-suite required check remain immutable; no waiver or product edit is authorized.

reviewedDisposition.environment: Same approved unsandboxed Windows x64 host, Node v24.13.1, AMD Ryzen 7 9700X 8-Core Processor, 9 samples and 2 warmups
reviewedDisposition.reason: A documented exception can explain attribution but cannot satisfy the required corepack pnpm bench command or Definition of Done while the command exits non-zero.

forbiddenPaths: packages/benchmarks/baseline.json or any change to canonical medians, invariants, warning threshold or failure threshold
packages/sim-core/**, packages/sim-protocol/**, packages/sim-worker/**, apps/**, renderer, UI, Electron, GameSession, content or other product/scenario-authority files
package.json, lockfiles, runtime dependencies, release, deploy or distribution files
coordination/artifacts/WM-0083/** or any historical benchmark artifact
benchmark workload, fixture size, invariant payload, default 9-sample/2-warmup count, suite omission, failure suppression, retry-until-pass or exception/waiver behavior
linked worktrees, concurrent dirty checkout comparisons or host/commit metadata omission
creating WM-0179 before a structured WM-0178 blocker names exact sim/product files, call paths, evidence and reason
claiming or unblocking WM-0177 or WM-0170 before the predecessor and review gates are complete
executionLane: qa-performance only; Spark is prohibited because cross-commit performance attribution, benchmark semantics and gate integrity require full evidence and independent review
conditionalProductRepair: WM-0179 may be architect-created only after WM-0178 blocks with exact non-harness files, call paths, suite evidence and reason. Until then it does not exist, is not a dependency and is not authorized.
singleWriterRule: WM-0178 is ready and unclaimed. Once claimed, qa-performance is the sole harness/evidence writer; reviewer remains read-only and independent. WM-0177 stays blocked and claimed by its original simulation-engineer, and WM-0170 stays blocked behind WM-0177.
